question.text: An option writer who sells a call option at a premium of Rs. 150 with strike price Rs. 18,000 will make a profit when:

answer.text: The underlying price stays at or below Rs. 18,150 (Strike + Premium) at expiry
The underlying price rises above Rs. 18,000 at expiry
The underlying price falls to zero at expiry
The underlying price equals exactly Rs. 18,000 at expiry

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Short call writer's BEP = Strike + Premium = 18,000 + 150 = 18,150 - Profit when S <= 18,150 at expiry - Maximum profit (Rs. 150) when S <= 18,000 (option expires worthless) - Between 18,000 and 18,150: partial profit (premium received > payoff to buyer) - Loss when S > 18,150
**The Trap:** The writer can still profit even if S is slightly above the strike, as long as S < BEP.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Writer profits when option expires OTM or loss is less than premium received.
**Key Takeaway:** Short call profit zone: S <= X + P. Max profit = P (when S <= X).
